a: Returns `true` when the execution modes being compared are different.

t: Messaging Between a Playground Page and the Always-On Live View
a: Display the results of running a playground page's code in a persistent live view.
Share playground data, manage live views, and control the execution of a playground.
A data storage container you use to persist information across different sessions.
A handler you use to send and receive messages between the always-on live view and its corresponding playground page.
The supported types for displaying for live views in playgrounds.
A UIKit view that's displayed as the live view.
An AppKit view that's displayed as the live view.
A UIKit view controller whose view is displayed as the live view.
An AppKit view controller whose view is displayed as the live view.
A protocol that ensures that views fit without obstruction within the Swift Playgrounds user interface.
A protocol that displays an instance as a live view in a playground.
An object you use to configure the state of a playground page and its live view.
The values you use to communicate whether a learner has passed, failed, or not yet finished the task on a page.
An assessment that communicates that a learner hasn't successfully completed a task.
An assessment that communicates that a learner has successfully completed a task.
The available speeds for executing the code on a playground page.
An execution mode that runs at the normal speed.
An execution mode that runs more quickly than usual.
An execution mode that runs at the fastest possible speed.
An execution mode that executes code statement by statement.
An execution mode that executes code statement by statement with extra pauses between each.
A proxy that facilitates message passing between the always-on live view and its corresponding playground page.
A delegate you use to receive messages from the always-on live view.
The types you can save in the key-value store or send in messages to live views.
A value that represents an array that contains [PlaygroundValue](/documentation/playgroundsupport/playgroundvalue) instances.
A value that represents a Boolean.
A value that represents raw data.
A value that represents a date.
A value that represents a mapping from strings to [PlaygroundValue](/documentation/playgroundsupport/playgroundvalue) instances.
A value that represents a floating-point number.
A value that represents an integer.
A value that represents a string.
A value that indicates whether a learner has passed, failed, or not yet finished the task on a page.
A reference to the key-value store for the current playground book.
The current playground page.
A delegate you use to handle messages from the live view.
The currently selected speed for executing the code on this playground page.
Terminates execution of the current playground page.
An object that persists data on a page between multiple sessions.
An object you use to add items to the Touch Bar of supported MacBook Pro models.
A live view that shows the result of running the code that’s on the current page.
Informs the handler that the connection between the proxy and the remote object was closed.
Informs the proxy that the connection between the proxy and the remote object was closed.
Informs the handler that the connection between the proxy and the remote object was closed.
Informs the handler that it has connected to a remote object.
Informs the proxy that it has connected to a remote object.
Informs the handler that it has connected to a remote object.
A layout guide you use to position content so that it's unobstructed by the Swift Playgrounds user interface.
A layout guide you use to position content so that it's unobstructed by the Swift Playgrounds user interface.
A layout guide you use to position content so that it's unobstructed by the Swift Playgrounds user interface.
A Boolean value that indicates whether indefinite execution is enabled.
The view or view controller used to render and manage the live view.
A representation of the live view that this object is a proxy for.
The path to the directory containing data shared between all playgrounds in Xcode.
Allows the handler to receive a live view message from a remote object.
Allows the proxy to receive a live view message from a remote object.
Allows the handler to receive a live view message from a remote object.
Tells the delegate that a message was received from the always-on live view.
Tells the delegate that it has disconnected from the always-on live view.
Sends a message to the always-on live view.
Allows the handler to send a message to a remote object.
Displays a view that shows the result of running the code that’s on the current page.
Displays a SwiftUI view that shows the result of running the code that’s on the current page.
Reads or stores the value associated with the given key in the key-value store.
The current contents of the playground page, including any user-entered text.
